event: presented to casualty with bleeding from the oral cavity
large clots in the oral cavity
endoscopy showed large eccentric friable mass lesion in hypopharynx
magnetic resonance angiography showed lesion encasing proximal 2 cm of external carotid artery
transfemoral supra-aortic digital subtraction angiogram showed active contrast extravasation from anterior wall of left external carotid artery
contrast tracking into pharynx, esophagus, and oral cavity
no active extravasation of dye post-embolization
tiny stump of left external carotid artery
cross occlusion preferred for durable hemostasis
predictors of CBS (pseudoaneurysm, vessel wall damage)
